data.title: Solana Co-Founder Proposes Inflation Adjustment to Enhance Network Security
data.description: According to Foresight News, Austin Federa, co-founder of DoubleZero and former head of strategy at the Solana Foundation, has suggested a change in Solana's inflation rate. Currently, Solana's annual inflation rate stands at 4.661%, with a reduction of 15% every 180 epochs. Federa has proposed increasing this reduction rate to 30% to optimize network security costs and reduce uncertainties associated with inflation. This proposal has been posted in the Solana Foundation's GitHub discussion area for further consideration.

data.title: Shardeum announces opening of airdrop pre-registration
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Layer1 blockchain Shardeum announced on the X platform that it has opened pre-registration for the SHM airdrop, and the website is now online. Eligible users can now check their eligibility by linking their wallet. It is reported that users who register within the first 7 days after the registration starts will receive allocations at TGE, and the SHM airdrop registration will be closed at 23:59 UTC on April 13, 2025.

data.title: Binance Futures Updates Leverage & Margin Tiers for Multiple USDⓈ-M Perpetual Contracts
data.description: Key Changes Effective March 14, 2025Binance Futures has announced updates to leverage and margin tiers for the following USDⓈ-M perpetual contracts:- AERGOUSDT- COMBOUSDT- LINAUSDT- VIDTUSDTThese changes will take effect on March 14, 2025, at 14:30 UTC and will impact existing positions. Traders using grid trading strategies are advised to adjust their positions accordingly.Summary of Leverage & Margin UpdatesAERGOUSDT Perpetual ContractLeverage Adjustments:Maximum leverage reduced to 8x for positions up to 20,000 USDT (previously varied across different tiers).Positions above 20,000 USDT remain at lower leverage levels, with 6x for 40,000-200,000 USDT and 5x for 200,000-400,000 USDT.Margin Rate Adjustments:Higher maintenance margin rates apply across all tiers.COMBOUSDT Perpetual ContractLeverage Adjustments:Maximum leverage reduced to 8x for positions up to 25,000 USDT (previously 9-10x).Higher margin requirements for positions above 400,000 USDT.Margin Rate Adjustments:Increased from 2.00% to 2.50% for lower-tier positions.LINAUSDT Perpetual ContractLeverage Adjustments:Maximum leverage reduced to 8x for positions up to 25,000 USDT.Lower leverage for larger positions, with 5x for 400,000-500,000 USDT.Margin Rate Adjustments:Increased margin requirements for higher positions.VIDTUSDT Perpetual ContractLeverage Adjustments:Maximum leverage reduced to 8x for positions up to 80,000 USDT.Positions above 80,000 USDT now limited to 7x leverage.Margin Rate Adjustments:Higher margin rates introduced for all positions.Important Notes for Traders- Existing positions will be affected. Traders must review their open positions and adjust leverage/margin settings to avoid liquidation risks.- Grid trading strategies may expire due to the leverage changes.- Ensure adequate margin levels before the update on March 14 at 14:30 UTC.

data.title: US lawmakers urge Treasury Secretary to stop trying to use Bitcoin as a strategic reserve
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Democratic Congressman Gerry Connolly of the U.S. House of Representatives Oversight Committee urged U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent to stop his attempt to create a strategic reserve of cryptocurrency. Connolly said that this move would not benefit the public and would only profit the president and his donors while exposing American taxpayers to great financial risks. Connolly also expressed concerns about U.S. President Trump's personal financial interests in cryptocurrencies and his dissatisfaction with the fact that the initiative was not approved by Congress. (DL News)

data.title: UK Crown Prosecution Service approves charges against UK official for allegedly stealing 50 BTC
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - The British Crown Prosecution Service has approved the National Crime Agency officer Paul Chowles, who stole 50 bitcoins during his previous investigation into organized cyber crime. At the time, these bitcoins were worth only 60,000 pounds (77,400 U.S. dollars), and are now worth about 3.2 million pounds (4.2 million U.S. dollars). (Theblock)

data.title: Crypto News: FTX Liquidated $1.5B in Three Arrows Capital Assets Two Weeks Before Hedge Fund’s Collapse
data.description: Key Takeaways:FTX secretly liquidated $1.53 billion in Three Arrows Capital (3AC) assets just two weeks before 3AC’s collapse in 2022.3AC has increased its claim against FTX from $120 million to $1.53 billion, arguing that it was unaware of the liquidations.Despite the missing $1.5 billion, analysts believe 3AC would still have collapsed due to its extensive debts.3AC creditors remain owed approximately $3.3 billion, with a British Virgin Islands court freezing $1.14 billion in assets from co-founders Kyle Davies and Su Zhu.FTX’s Undisclosed Liquidation of 3AC AssetsNewly revealed court documents show that FTX liquidated $1.53 billion worth of Three Arrows Capital (3AC) assets in early 2022, just two weeks before the hedge fund collapsed. This challenges the previous belief that 3AC’s failure was entirely market-driven.3AC, once valued at over $10 billion, collapsed following the May 2022 crypto crash, which saw Bitcoin (BTC) fall to $16,000. The hedge fund had borrowed from more than 20 major institutions, engaging in highly leveraged trades that unraveled as market conditions deteriorated.However, recently discovered evidence suggests that FTX may have significantly contributed to 3AC’s downfall. The exchange liquidated 3AC’s holdings without notifying the firm, and 3AC is now pushing for a full $1.53 billion claim in FTX’s bankruptcy case."3AC says it only recently discovered evidence that FTX liquidated $1.53 billion of its assets just two weeks before it went into liquidation—far more than the $120M originally claimed," noted “Mbottjer,” a pseudonymous co-founder of FTX Creditor.Legal Battle Over 3AC's Missing FundsA U.S. court has now ruled in favor of 3AC, allowing it to expand its claim against FTX. The hedge fund contends that FTX’s own bankruptcy proceedings kept the liquidation hidden from them.In a separate legal action, a British Virgin Islands court froze $1.14 billion of assets linked to 3AC co-founders Kyle Davies and Su Zhu. Despite this, liquidators Teneo estimate that 3AC creditors are still owed $3.3 billion.Davies has disputed claims that he and Su Zhu were uncooperative in the liquidation process, stating that Teneo exaggerated their allegations.Would 3AC Have Survived With the Missing $1.5 Billion?Despite the large sum involved, analysts believe that even if 3AC had retained the $1.5 billion, it would not have been enough to prevent collapse.“Even if they had the additional $1.5 billion in 2022, they still would not have been able to meet creditor claims or debt repayments,” said Nicolai Sondergaard, research analyst at Nansen.The uncertainty remains as to whether 3AC will successfully recover the full $1.53 billion from FTX’s bankruptcy case. Analysts predict that 3AC will receive more than its initial $120M claim, but securing the full amount is unlikely.Market ReactionsThe revelations have sparked renewed scrutiny over FTX’s role in the crypto market’s 2022 collapse. As FTX’s bankruptcy proceedings continue, the outcome of 3AC’s claim could impact creditor distributions and shape the future of crypto bankruptcy settlements, according to Cointelegraph.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Spot Funds Experience Mixed Inflows and Outflows
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, recent data from Coinglass reveals significant movements in cryptocurrency spot funds over the past 24 hours. XRP led the net inflow list with $32.31 million, followed by TRUMP with $11.14 million, BNB with $10.05 million, and PEPE with $6.19 million.Conversely, DOGE topped the net outflow list, experiencing a withdrawal of $31.53 million. BTC followed with $24.01...

data.title: Forbes: Trump administration wants to buy as much Bitcoin as possible
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Trump issued an executive order to establish a strategic reserve of Bitcoin in the United States, but it failed to meet market expectations, causing the price of Bitcoin to be on the verge of a sharp drop. Since then, the price of Bitcoin has fallen to around $80,000 per Bitcoin. It is reported that Bo Hines, the White House digital asset policy adviser, told participants at a closed-door roundtable hosted by the Bitcoin Policy Institute that the Trump administration hopes to acquire as much Bitcoin as possible. A White House official added that any such acquisition would be "conducted in a budget-neutral manner and would not cost taxpayers a penny." 
According to reports, those attending the private meeting included U.S. Senators Cynthia Lummis, Bill Hagerty, Bernie Moreno, as well as Strategy founder Michael Saylor, Marathon Digital CEO Fred Thiel, and Anchorage Digital CEO Nathan McCauley. (Forbes)

data.title: California man sentenced to 87 months in prison for laundering money using Bitcoin
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: A California man was sentenced for using Bitcoin to launder the proceeds of ecstasy sold on the dark web. According to police, the man imported ecstasy from Germany and then sold it on various dark web markets, accepting Bitcoin payments. He then exchanged the Bitcoin stored in the seller's account for US dollars and laundered the black money through "hundreds of transactions and dozens of financial accounts." The man was sentenced to 87 months in federal prison after pleading guilty to conspiracy to launder money and conspiracy to operate an unlicensed money transmission business. (Decrypt)

data.title: Future Trends in Crypto: Focus on Utility and Sustainable Revenue
data.description: According to PANews, Maria Shen, a partner at Electric Capital, highlighted that memecoins have concentrated speculative activities within a specific area, allowing other segments of the crypto market to focus on fundamentals. Looking ahead, sectors such as DeFi and infrastructure are expected to prioritize utility, sustainable revenue, and token value pathways. Shen anticipates that yield-generating tokens, stringent capital conditions, regulatory clarity, and consumer-friendly products will become prevalent trends, with projects increasingly centered around practicality.
